clusters.headline: South Africa: Power Utility Eskom Says Koeberg Remains Safe - South African News Briefs - July 22, 2026
Rubio says US open to talks on Middle East conflict, but Iran not serious
clusters.summary:
Rubio says US open to talks on Middle East conflict, but Iran not serious

    
    

        
    
                US State Secretary Marco Rubio said that Washington is willing to engage, negotiate and settle differences with Iran regarding the ongoing conflict in the region.&nb

clusters.headline: Faishal's online replies 'objectively inappropriate and questionable'; he was given fair hearing: Shanmugam
clusters.summary: Minister for Home Affairs K Shanmugam acknowledged that "many are wondering what happened; what were the standards of conduct that were applied; why Faishal decided to resign".

clusters.headline: Can India’s national space agency keep its top scientists on board as the private spacetech sector booms?
clusters.summary: As India’s private spacetech sector takes off, competition for its most experienced scientists is raising questions about how the country can build a thriving commercial space industry without hollowing out the institution that helped make it possible.

clusters.headline: ‘Hopes are dashed’: Network School members express frustration after Forest City campus ordered to close
clusters.summary: Some members of the tech startup community told CNA they are cutting short their stay in Johor and returning home after authorities ordered the Network School to cease operations immediately.

clusters.headline: Singapore to expand air traffic control workforce by 40%, with S$20,000 sign-on bonus for new recruits
clusters.summary: Licensed air traffic control officers can earn about S$120,000 a year after five years of experience.

clusters.headline: Zimbabwe: Govt Says 15,000 of 108,000 Returnees From South Africa Already Registered for Employment
clusters.summary: [New Zimbabwe] Government says more than 15,000 Zimbabweans who have returned from South Africa have already been registered for employment, as the number of returnees under the ongoing repatriation programme surpassed 108,000.

clusters.headline: Police officers arrested for allegedly seeking roadside bribe from Nigeria's anti-corruption boss
clusters.summary: They are accused of stopping Musa Aliyu at a fake roadblock and telling him to drive to a cash machine.

clusters.headline: Fresh South China Sea tensions add urgency to ASEAN-China code of conduct talks: Analysts
clusters.summary: The latest flashpoint has cast a shadow over this week’s ASEAN Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Manila, which is also being attended by United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi.

clusters.headline: Changi Control Tower to undergo upgrading as part of S$4 billion air navigation overhaul
clusters.summary: Changi Airport is also progressing towards three-runway operations by the end of the decade.

clusters.headline: Mauritania: UN Refugee Agency Reports 144 Migrants Dead or Missing Off Mauritanian Coast
clusters.summary: [UN News] The UN's refugee agency (UNHCR) reports that 144 migrants attempting to reach Europe are dead or missing due to a series of "tragic maritime incidents" off the coast of Mauritania last week.

clusters.headline: Shock and anger after top Ghanaian politician sentenced to 20 years for illegal mining
clusters.summary: Bernard Antwi Boasiako, known as "Chairman Wontumi", was also fined more than $10,000 (£7,450).
